author: Robert Carr
description: A hot egg sandwich with butter-toasted wheat bread, warmed chorizo or salami, soft folded egg, cheddar, pickles, greens, onion, and a small Dijon burger-style sauce.
ingredients: 1 to 1 1/2 ounces chorizo, salami, or salchichon-style salami, thinly sliced
A few very thin onion slices
2 to 3 dill pickle slices, patted dry
Small handful baby arugula or lettuce, patted dry
1 teaspoon pickle brine from the jar, divided
instructions: Stir the mayonnaise, Dijon mustard, ketchup, and 1/2 teaspoon pickle brine together in a small bowl.
Toss the arugula or lettuce with the onion slices, extra-virgin olive oil, and remaining 1/2 teaspoon pickle brine.
Beat the egg with a fork in a small bowl until no streaks of white remain.
Warm a nonstick skillet over medium-low heat for 1 minute.
Add the chorizo or salami and cook for 45 to 60 seconds per side, just until the edges curl and a little fat renders. Transfer to a plate.
Melt 1/2 tablespoon butter in the skillet and toast both bread slices for 1 to 2 minutes per side, until lightly golden. Transfer the toast to a plate.
Lower the heat to low and melt the remaining 1/2 tablespoon butter in the skillet.
Pour in the beaten egg and gently push it into a rough square or rectangle as it sets.
When the egg is just set on top, fold it once or twice to fit the bread, place the cheddar on top, and cover the skillet for 30 to 45 seconds until the cheese softens.
Spread the Dijon sauce on the toast.
Build the sandwich with the cheesy folded egg, warmed chorizo or salami, pickles, and dressed greens.
Close the sandwich, press gently, cut in half, and eat right away.
notes: The egg and butter give the sandwich structure and richness while the pickles and greens keep it from feeling heavy.
Spicy pecan cold-smoked chorizo pairs well with cheddar, butter, egg, and pickles.
Cook the egg gently. A dry browned egg makes the sandwich taste flat, while a soft folded egg makes it taste rich.
The dressed greens and pickles go in after cooking so they stay crisp and bright.
personalNotes: This was okay, might remove it from the recipe collection. I was trying to make something from the ingredients I had as a quick midnight snack. I am not sure I'd cook this again with the chorizo salami, maybe I'd also cook the onions, and not use generic pp&j sandwich wheat bread.
title: Chorizo Cheddar Egg Sandwich
